Improved Invisibility to Undead  

Description

1: Improved Invis Undead

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 75 Skill: Divination
Casting Time: 4 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 100
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Deletable: No Blockable: Yes
Focusable: Yes Dispellable: Yes
Interruptable: Yes Short Buff Box: No
Target Type: Self Spell Type: Beneficial
Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: You feel your skin tingle.
Cast on other: Soandso fades a little.
Effect Fades: Your skin stops tingling.

Game Description

Cloaks your body in a light aura, rendering you invisible to most undead creatures for 10.0 mins.

Items with this effect

  • Spell: Improved Invis vs. Undead

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Improved Invis vs. Undead
ClassLevel
NEC50
SHD61
PAL63
CLR48
Expansion:

Duration:
10.0 mins
  • Comments (24)
  • Sold By (4)
  • Dropped By (48)
  • Crafted (1)
  • Screenshots

Merchants:


The Plane of Knowledge
  • Cavalier Cerakor - 51pp 9gp 1sp 8cp
  • Heretic Elirev - 51pp 9gp 1sp 8cp
  • Reaver Muron - 51pp 9gp 1sp 8cp
  • Vicar Thiran - 51pp 9gp 1sp 8cp
Add Image
Post Comment
Advanced Reasearch Book
QuoteReply
# Nov 03 2005 at 5:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593967395591
9 posts
The advanced research book you are talking about (though by now have already found, but others might not have) is from a guy in Gunthak named Researcher Alvarikat loc +1367, +80. When you find him, as stated above, your research must be over 100, and say to him I am a Necromancer (or Wiz, Mage or Chanter) and he will give you the new book.
▲ Page top
Actual Name
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2005 at 3:08 PM Rating: Decent
dafdaf
View User Forum
Scholar
1,273 posts
For those trying to search in the bazaar for this spell, this spell scroll is actually named:
Spell: Improved Invis vs Undead
▲ Page top
Left out. . .?
QuoteReply
# Jun 28 2005 at 9:29 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592342345933
17 posts

What gives?
A wizard can research the spell and can't use it?
The only protection he'll get is maximizing the 5 min window he gets from the regular Invis to Undead?
Wheres my aspirin. . . ?
▲ Page top
Left out. . .?
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2008 at 4:58 PM Rating: Good
UnholyCzars
View User Forum
Sage
756 posts
Wizards get a different spell named Invisibility VERSUS Undead. It drops in Wakening Lands by Holgresh.
____________________________
Fight smarter not harder.
▲ Page top
Left out. . .?
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2008 at 4:55 PM Rating: Good
UnholyCzars

Sage

Wizards get a different spell named Invisibility VERSUS Undead.

▲ Page top
RE: Left out. . .?
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2005 at 6:38 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592266080663
140 posts
The reason you can research it is because it is used by WIS casting classes, therefor all int casting classes may research it, though most of them may not scribe it.
▲ Page top
RE: Left out. . .?
QuoteReply
# Dec 04 2005 at 3:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591823673105
30 posts

QuoteReply
# Apr 19 2006 at 7:34 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592271886428
145 posts
Ummm, no your not on bad info but pay attention re-re-******. Clerics and paladins both can use THIS particular spell also, that's the WIS classes he's talkng about. A-DUH!
▲ Page top
what !? Left out in the open . . ?
QuoteReply
# Jun 28 2005 at 9:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592342345933
17 posts
▲ Page top
found
QuoteReply
# Apr 18 2005 at 8:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257853442
13 posts
Found 2 of these on same Frosticube in Icewall Keep
▲ Page top
targetting
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2004 at 10:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592763806772
346 posts
Don't be confused by spell info however. It may say "your target", but it is a self only spell. This site is right about its information, eqs scroll info isn't in game isn't.

To cast invis vs undead on others, you still must use basic invis vs undead.
▲ Page top
Why its called improved...
QuoteReply
# Aug 22 2003 at 11:03 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591777534728
21 posts
Improved Invisibility spells have a set duration and won't drop at unexpected times.
▲ Page top
question
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2003 at 1:08 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249641124
79 posts
i know with dungeon's and dragons "improved invisibility" lets you attack and remain invis.is this spell similar?
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2003 at 12:38 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592256351560
48 posts
No. While a lot of EQ is based off of D&D, there are a few differences. In EQ anytime you are invis (spell or hide) and you cast a spell, attack, get hit by a mob, or loot something you become visable. If it was like that the price for see invis items would shoot through the roof on PvP servers.

Edited, Thu Nov 6 12:41:01 2003
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2004 at 6:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591792696375
Scholar
2,342 posts
One case where you can remain invis... a bard's Fading Memories ability.
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2004 at 7:40 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592760550389
167 posts
Fading Memories does 2 things:
1) removes the bard from all hate/rampage/etc. lists of all mobs.
2) makes the bard invis til he zones OR does any of the things that normally drop invis (attack, cast, loot, etc.)
▲ Page top
Dropped
QuoteReply
# Mar 24 2003 at 7:03 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250166807
Scholar
69 posts
This dropped in Plane of Growth last night. Can't remember what it dropped off of, but we were killing bears (that weren't called bears), sanguine kodiaks and spirit flux wolves. Didn't kill trees or anything that looked human.
We had 2 groups with 4 people each. Both groups had a shammy (no clerics) and we were 47-54.
Zone appeared to have been cleared shortly before we got there, but there were enough leftovers to keep us busy for a couple hours.
My 51 necro got this, and 2 BDs dropped as well as stonewood mail and some class armor.
My guess is that its a rare drop off of 60+ mobs, but I can definitely confirm that it can drop in PoG.
▲ Page top
Reseacrhable?
QuoteReply
# Mar 19 2003 at 9:36 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759891885
View User Forum
19 posts
Does anyone know how to get the book in which to scribe spells? Must be master in research, but i dont have that skill avail to me - anyone try this yet.

Do you have to have an INT caster do this for you>?


Edited, Thu Mar 20 07:24:36 2003
▲ Page top
RE: Reseacrhable?
QuoteReply
# Oct 14 2003 at 2:57 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591792177509
View User Forum
56 posts
I don't recall the name of the guy, but there is someone in the Gulf of Gunthak, in one of the huts by the lighthouse. He is in the hut, as well as his boy-servant or something. You hail him and say "I am a necromancer" or whatever class you are, and he will give you your advanced research tome--but only if your research is over 100 already. That should do ya. You need this book to make swatches for high-end tailored robes (robe of misery, robe of tempest) as well.
▲ Page top
RE: Reseacrhable?
QuoteReply
# Apr 18 2003 at 12:07 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776013048
View User Forum
263 posts
Only Int casters can research. The researchable wis-caster spells must be researched by an Int caster.
▲ Page top
One drop...
QuoteReply
# Mar 15 2003 at 7:53 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253222814
16 posts
Dropped off Crystaline Wurms in the caves in Great Divide. Got 3 to drop in 4 hour camp. Possibly off the Ancient as well. fyi
